name: vibe3d
description: simple 3d modeller inspired MODO and LigthWave
_comment-assimp-static: assimp is static-linked via subConfigurations.bindbc-assimp6=static. ALL build+link config (per-platform preBuildCommands that build the minimal OBJ/glTF/FBX libassimp, lflags by explicit archive path, and stdc++/m) now lives in the bindbc-assimp6 `static` configuration — a clean clone auto-builds it, no manual prerequisite. The base BindAssimp_Static version below is for vibe3d's own source/io/* guards.

profile-fp._comment: Profiling build: -gs forces frame pointers so perf --call-graph fp produces clean stacks without DWARF unwind cost. Use: `dub build --build=profile-fp` then `perf record -F 999 --call-graph fp -p $(pgrep vibe3d) sleep 5`.

perf._comment: Perf-harness build: optimized + inlined release with PerfProbe instrumentation active. releaseMode strips bounds-checks/asserts off the hot per-vertex loops so the timers measure the tool, not bounds-checking. The default build defines no PerfProbe version, so g_perf calls compile to no-ops. Use: `dub build --build=perf --compiler=ldc2`.

configurations.name: modeling
configurations._comment: Default. Modeling-only build — no IPR, no Cycles/RPR runtime, no D-Cycles/D-RadeonProRender dependencies. Fast compile, no extra system libs needed. All code under source/render/* and source/material/* is excluded from the build (gated by `version (WithRender)`).

configurations.name: with-render
configurations._comment: Opt-in build with two render backends (Cycles via D-Cycles, RPR via D-RadeonProRender). The default `modeling` config never references these so `dub build` works on machines that have neither repo. Reproducibility comes from `dub.selections.json` (committed) which captures the resolved git SHA — CI builds against it, anyone else fetches the same. For active backend development, register a local override once: `dub add-local ~/Code/D-RadeonProRender && dub add-local ~/Code/D-Cycles`. dub then resolves the local working tree at branch ~master instead of re-cloning, so a small shim fix doesn't trigger a multi-GB Blender LFS pull or a full Cycles rebuild. D-Cycles builds its C shim via CMake (Blender LFS submodule + optional CUDA/OptiX); D-RadeonProRender is dynamic-loaded bindbc. See doc/shader_framework_plan.md.
